methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $231M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Pragmatic Semiconductor is a Series D chip design company within a competitive frontier-tech cohort. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers spanning similar and adjacent stages, including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, and Cerebras.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the company's $231M total raised as a proxy baseline, applying a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier for Series D progression, implying $346M. Peer-median triangulation uses three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: SambaNova Systems at $5.0B (Series D), Tenstorrent at $2.7B (Series D), and DEEPX at $529M (Series C). The median across this set is $2.7B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue data, which is typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated analysis across both methods yields a valuation range of $295M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.5B. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty: stage-progression conservatism versus peer-median upside depends heavily on whether Pragmatic's technology trajectory and market position more closely resemble the lower-funded DEEPX or the billion-plus-valued peers. Capital efficiency and revenue traction remain critical tiebreakers.
